title: Error when trying to get container running on Debian11 -> Error:  /usr/bin/slirp4netns failed: "open(\"/dev/net/tun\"): Permission denied
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Hello community,
I'm trying to get a podman container e.x. "hello-world" running on Debian 11. Always when I try to get the container up an running, I receive the mentioned error message:-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. install podman

2. run container : podman run hello-world

**Describe the results you received:**
Error thrown:
ERRO[0001] Error preparing container caa955a1419df75eb53018b35d4a0d891759436c5057b55422c4286e7c35120c: /usr/bin/slirp4netns failed: "open(\"/dev/net/tun\"): Permission denied\nWARNING: Support for seccomp is experimental\nchild failed(1)\nWARNING: Support for seccomp is experimental\n" 
Error: error resolving storage path for container caa955a1419df75eb53018b35d4a0d891759436c5057b55422c4286e7c35120c: lstat /home/dennis/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/d05bc36a0a8b23cdb9a1b5c56aa371406c81ae204cb52f8d82f877e11c1e39de/merged: invalid argument

Container not getting up and running
**Describe the results you expected:**
I'd expect to get the container up and running

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**


```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.15.15
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

**Output of `sudo ls -al /dev/net/tun`:**

```
[sudo] Passwort für dennis: 
crw-rw-rw- 1 root root 10, 200 21. Jun 18:59 /dev/net/tun
```

also tun module is loaded
**Output of `sudo lsmod |grep tun`:** -> 

```
tun                     4242  -2
vznetstat               4242  -2 tun,ip_vznetstat,ip6_vznetstat
```

Thank you so much for your support in advance.

comments.body: Any chance access is being blocked by SELinux / Apparmor?

Hi @mheon thanks for your reply, but I don‘t think so, because output of `podman info --debug` saying 
```
security:
    **apparmorEnabled: false**
    capabilities: CAP_AUDIT_WRITE,CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_MKNOD,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_NET_RAW,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    **selinuxEnabled: false** 
```
But are they enabled on the system itself? That tells us that Podman is not enforcing them for containers, but not that the system overall is does not have them enabled.
oh, I was interpreting that this information is regarding Debian (system overall) not regarding the container environment. I‘ll check that, thanks

oh, unfortunately it seems to be, that neither apparmor or selinux are causing the issue:

`sudo getenforce
->
Disabled
`

`sudo aa-status
->
apparmor not present.
`
How did you install Podman? Debian 11's own packages have Podman 3.0.1 still.

oh yes, you are right. In this case I used the repository from:
https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/
I used the instruction from: 
https://computingforgeeks.com/how-to-install-podman-on-debian/
for that.
But exactly the same issue occurs when I‘m trying to use it with Podman 3.0.1 from debian repo.

Do rootful containers work?
`$ sudo  podman run hello-world`
Does 
`$ podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map `
work?

Is there anything strange about the setup of your homedir?
@giuseppe Could this be a fuse-overlayfs problem?

Output of 
> sudo podman run hello-world


```

[sudo] Passwort für dennis: 
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-ptp.conflist: [failed to find plugin "ptp" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "portmap" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "firewall" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]] 
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-ptp.conflist: [failed to find plugin "ptp" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "portmap" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "firewall" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]] 
ERRO[0000] error loading cached network config: network "podman" not found in CNI cache 
WARN[0000] falling back to loading from existing plugins on disk 
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-ptp.conflist: [failed to find plugin "ptp" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "portmap" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "firewall" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]] 
ERRO[0000] Error tearing down partially created network namespace for container e34ab82db5066eee6205fd540e1ce47761c9b03b5531c037e3bbf14a0b96cd3a: CNI network "podman" not found 
ERRO[0000] error unmounting /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/e0c8562358da61a0a06e8115dc45490f9bd2c74304031422b0de98cd2654e6b8/merged: invalid argument 
ERRO[0000] Error preparing container e34ab82db5066eee6205fd540e1ce47761c9b03b5531c037e3bbf14a0b96cd3a: error configuring network namespace for container e34ab82db5066eee6205fd540e1ce47761c9b03b5531c037e3bbf14a0b96cd3a: CNI network "podman" not found 
Error: error mounting storage for container e34ab82db5066eee6205fd540e1ce47761c9b03b5531c037e3bbf14a0b96cd3a: error creating overlay mount to /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/e0c8562358da61a0a06e8115dc45490f9bd2c74304031422b0de98cd2654e6b8/mergedmount_data="nodev,metacopy=on,lowerdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/l/GPFYLVDK5TLO34BSINIYFJ7YMV,upperdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/e0c8562358da61a0a06e8115dc45490f9bd2c74304031422b0de98cd2654e6b8/diff,workdir=/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/e0c8562358da61a0a06e8115dc45490f9bd2c74304031422b0de98cd2654e6b8/work": invalid argument
```

and output of

> $ podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map 

`WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /home/pod/.config/cni/net.d/87-podman.conflist: [failed to find plugin "bridge" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "portmap" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "firewall" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "tuning" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]] 
WARN[0000] Error validating CNI config file /home/pod/.config/cni/net.d/proxy_default.conflist: [failed to find plugin "bridge" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "portmap" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "firewall" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin] failed to find plugin "tuning" in path [/usr/local/libexec/cni /usr/libexec/cni /usr/local/lib/cni /usr/lib/cni /opt/cni/bin]] 
         0       1001          1
         1     165536      65536
`
I don’t know how to interpret this - do you know?

If I do 

> podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map

with a user which is in sudo group, I get:

> podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map 

`

         0       1000          1
         1     100000      65536
`

Can you be more specific what you mean with: 
> Is there anything strange about the setup of your homedir?

can I check this somehow?

does `/dev/net/tun` exist?

You may need to load the kernel module

> does `/dev/net/tun` exist?
> 
> You may need to load the kernel module

yes it exists:

```
Output of sudo ls -al /dev/net/tun:

[sudo] Passwort für dennis: 
crw-rw-rw- 1 root root 10, 200 21. Jun 18:59 /dev/net/tun
```
and also tun module is loaded

```
Output of sudo lsmod |grep tun: ->

tun                     4242  -2
vznetstat               4242  -2 tun,ip_vznetstat,ip6_vznetstat
```
This is feeling a lot like the kernel is out of date or does not have support for some of the overlay fields we are using?
also `Version:      3.4.2` is ancient.  Could you try with podman from upstream?

> also Version: 3.4.2 is ancient. Could you try with podman from upstream?

Oh, maybe that made some progress. I installed version 4.1.1 and trying to run ex. hello-world. Now I get a new error...

`podman run hello-world
-> 
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: unable to create kernel-style whiteout: operation not permitted
`
Thank you for your support so far. Do you have maybe one more idea how I can resolve the "new" problem?
what is the content of `/home/dennis/.config/containers/storage.conf`?  I'd suggest to delete it and use the default configuration generated by podman

> what is the content of `/home/dennis/.config/containers/storage.conf`? I'd suggest to delete it and use the default configuration generated by podman

This is strange... 

`/home/dennis/.config/containers/storage.conf`

does not exist.

oh it is working now.
 I could get it working installing fuse-overlayfs.
And after that I needed to create /dev/fuse again with:

`sudo mknod /dev/fuse -m 0666 c 10 229
`

Thank you so much for your support and effort.
then I am closing since it works as expected.  If fuse-overlayfs is not available, Podman tries only native overlay without fallback to fuse-overlayfs
